steps.title: Identify what “merely fictionalized” signals
steps.content: The strongest clue is that readers might believe Bronte “merely fictionalized true events”—where “merely” means the real events are central, with minimal creative alteration, not original invention.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
In a field valuing creativity and inventiveness, if readers believe the work is merely real events fictionalized (not original), this would suggest the work is less of an imaginative achievement than it actually is—exactly what choice A states.

The other choices address unrelated matters: identifying other characters' real-world sources (B), Bronte's own claims (C), or unmentioned critical disagreement (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
